settings.title: Workspace Settings
settings.subtitle: Tune the shell, editor, and runtime defaults
settings.description: The interface supports both dark and light shells, while Monaco keeps its own theme pipeline for code editing. Changes are saved automatically.
settings.footer: Settings persist locally across desktop and web sessions.
about.title: About
about.description: Product identity, version metadata, and release entry points for this build.
about.hero.copy: Lingua keeps fast multi-language experiments inside one focused editor shell.
about.field.versionHint: Desktop reads the packaged app version; web falls back to bundled metadata.
about.field.buildDateHint: Approximate bundle timestamp for the current build.
about.links.label: Project links
about.links.hint: Open the canonical repository, website, or license document.
about.actions.label: Release actions
about.actions.hint: Jump into updates, release notes, or onboarding from the identity section.
whatsNew.title: What's New
whatsNew.subtitle: Release notes and recent changes
whatsNew.description: Review the latest shipped changes first, then expand older release notes when you need more context.
whatsNew.empty: No release notes are bundled with this build yet.
tour.step.editor.title: Editor workspace
tour.step.editor.text: This is the main coding surface. Templates, inline results, and runtime markers all stay anchored to the editor so you can iterate without leaving the workspace.
tour.step.run.title: Run the active file
tour.step.run.text: Click Run to continue. The tour waits for a real execution here so you can see the feedback loop instead of skipping past it.
tour.step.console.title: Console feedback
tour.step.console.text: Logs, warnings, errors, and timing badges land here. When execution emits structured output, Lingua keeps the console and inline panel in sync.
tour.step.explorer.title: Project explorer
tour.step.explorer.text: Use the explorer to open folders, create files, and keep tabs tied to the working tree instead of isolated scratch files.
tour.step.toolbar.title: Toolbar controls
tour.step.toolbar.text: Quick-open, command palette, snippets, console visibility, and settings all live in the top toolbar so the core workflow stays one click away.
tour.step.snippets.title: Snippet library
tour.step.snippets.text: Save useful code from the active tab into reusable snippets, then reopen it later in a new tab or insert it into the current file.
tour.step.commandPalette.title: Command palette
tour.step.commandPalette.text: The command palette is the fastest way to navigate templates, snippets, layout changes, and actions. Open it anytime with Cmd+Shift+P or Ctrl+Shift+P.
appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Lingua supports a dark-first shell and a refined light mode without changing your editor runtime settings.
appearance.theme.dark.label: Dark
appearance.theme.dark.description: High-contrast graphite shell for focused coding sessions.
appearance.theme.light.label: Light
appearance.theme.light.description: Warm paper workspace with calmer contrast and stronger daylight readability.
layout.title: Layout
layout.description: Choose how the editor and console share space inside the main workstation.
layout.preset.horizontal.label: Horizontal Split
layout.preset.vertical.label: Vertical Split
layout.preset.editorOnly.label: Editor Only
editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Keep the Monaco surface aligned with how you read code and how aggressively you want automatic safeguards.
editor.theme.label: Editor theme
editor.theme.hint: Picks the Monaco theme. The shell polarity follows this choice unless the sync toggle is off.
editor.syncShellWithEditorTheme.label: Match shell to editor theme
editor.syncShellWithEditorTheme.hint: When on, picking a light editor theme flips the console and run-result panels to light. Turn off to keep the shell theme independent.
editor.fontFamily.label: Font family
editor.fontFamily.hint: Use a coding typeface that matches your workflow.
editor.fontLigatures.label: Font ligatures
editor.fontLigatures.hint: Render combined glyphs for operators such as =>, !=, and >=.
editor.fontLigatures.unavailableHint: The selected font does not ship programmer ligatures.
editor.fontSize.label: Font size
editor.fontSize.hint: Adjust the editor scale without touching the shell density.
editor.lineNumbers.label: Line numbers
editor.lineNumbers.hint: Keep gutter references visible while editing.
editor.wordWrap.label: Word wrap
editor.wordWrap.hint: Wrap long lines inside the viewport.
editor.minimap.label: Minimap
editor.minimap.hint: Show the code overview strip on the right edge.
editor.loopProtection.label: Loop protection
editor.loopProtection.hint: Stops runaway JS and TS loops before they lock the renderer.
editor.maxIterations.label: Max iterations
editor.maxIterations.hint: Caps the injected loop guard for inline JS, TS, and Python runners.
editor.restoreSession.label: Reopen last session
editor.restoreSession.hint: Restore tabs from the previous session on restart.
editor.formatOnSave.label: Format on save
editor.formatOnSave.hint: Run Prettier for JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, and CSS, gofmt / rustfmt for Go and Rust, and ruff (falling back to black) for Python before writing the file.
editor.formatOnSave.parseError: Could not format {{name}} — saved the original content.
editor.formatOnSave.binaryMissing: Formatter binary missing for {{name}} — saved the original content.
editor.formatOnSave.webUnavailable: Formatting {{name}} is desktop-only — saved the original content.
editor.formatOnSave.unknownError: Format-on-save failed for {{name}} — saved the original content.
settings.themePreset.label: Theme preset
settings.themePreset.hint: Export your editor theme, fonts, and layout as shareable JSON, or import a preset someone sent you.
settings.themePreset.error.bridgeMissing: File dialogs are not available in this build.
settings.themePreset.error.invalidJson: The selected file is not valid JSON.
settings.themePreset.error.invalidShape: The preset file is missing required fields.
settings.themePreset.error.unsupportedVe: This preset was created by a newer or older version of Lingua.
settings.themePreset.error.readFailed: Could not read the selected preset.
settings.themePreset.error.writeFailed: Could not write the preset to disk.
updates.title: Updates
updates.description: Desktop update status is surfaced here; web builds show capability limits explicitly.
updates.status.label: Status
updates.status.hint: Review the current desktop update state and any runtime messages.
updates.actions.label: Actions
updates.actions.hint: These controls stay disabled when updates are not supported.
updates.message.webUnavailable: Automatic updates are not available in the web version.
plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Local plugin discovery is surfaced as an operational panel rather than a marketplace promise.
plugins.directory.label: Local plugin directory
language.label: Language
language.hint: Choose the display language for the Lingua interface.

emptyState.headline: Run experiments fast, keep the workspace calm, and stay inside one editor.
emptyState.description: Start a blank file, open a language template, or jump straight to a snippet. The shell is tuned for tight feedback loops and native desktop execution when you need it.
emptyState.startingPoints.title: Starting Points
emptyState.startingPoints.description: Open a proven template and iterate.
quickOpen.empty.noProject: No files open. Open a project to browse files.

utilities.title: Built-in utilities
utilities.description: Keep the common format, decode, and inspection tasks inside Lingua instead of jumping to external tools.
utilities.tool.json.label: Open JSON Formatter
utilities.tool.json.description: Format, validate, minify, and inspect JSON payloads.
utilities.tool.json.title: JSON formatter and validator
utilities.tool.json.panelDescription: Paste JSON and keep it readable, compact, and structurally valid.
utilities.tool.json.viewerDescription: Inspect parsed objects and arrays without leaving the utility panel.
utilities.tool.json.error: The current value is not valid JSON.
utilities.tool.json.empty: Enter JSON to inspect its parsed structure.
utilities.tool.base64.label: Open Base64 Encoder
utilities.tool.base64.description: Encode plain text to Base64 or decode Base64 payloads back to text.
utilities.tool.base64.title: Base64 encode and decode
utilities.tool.base64.panelDescription: Switch modes to transform text in either direction without leaving Lingua.
utilities.tool.base64.error: The current value is not valid Base64.
utilities.tool.url.label: Open URL Encoder
utilities.tool.url.description: Encode or decode URL components and query-string fragments.
utilities.tool.url.title: URL encode and decode
utilities.tool.url.panelDescription: Escape or restore URL-safe text for query params and path segments.
utilities.tool.url.error: The current value is not valid URL-encoded text.
utilities.tool.uuid.label: Open UUID Generator
utilities.tool.uuid.description: Generate fresh UUID v4 identifiers for mock data or config values.
utilities.tool.uuid.title: UUID generator
utilities.tool.uuid.panelDescription: Create a small batch of random identifiers you can copy into code, fixtures, or docs.
utilities.tool.hash.label: Open Hash Generator
utilities.tool.hash.description: Create SHA-1 or SHA-256 digests for the current input.
utilities.tool.hash.title: Hash generator
utilities.tool.hash.panelDescription: Generate deterministic digests for strings, payload samples, or fixtures.
utilities.tool.hash.error: Hash generation is unavailable in this runtime.
utilities.tool.timestamp.label: Open Timestamp Converter
utilities.tool.timestamp.description: Convert Unix timestamps and date strings into readable date formats.
utilities.tool.timestamp.title: Unix timestamp converter
utilities.tool.timestamp.panelDescriptio: Enter seconds, milliseconds, or a date string to inspect multiple time representations.
utilities.tool.timestamp.outputsDescript: Compare canonical timestamp formats without leaving the editor shell.
utilities.tool.timestamp.error: Enter a Unix timestamp or a date string.
utilities.tool.jwt.label: Open JWT Decoder
utilities.tool.jwt.description: Decode JWT headers and payload claims without verifying signatures.
utilities.tool.jwt.title: JWT decoder
utilities.tool.jwt.panelDescription: Inspect token metadata and claims quickly while keeping the signature untouched.
utilities.tool.jwt.headerDescription: Algorithm and token metadata extracted from the header segment.
utilities.tool.jwt.payloadDescription: Claims extracted from the payload segment.
utilities.tool.jwt.empty: Enter a JWT to inspect its decoded JSON payload.
utilities.tool.jwt.error: The current token is not a valid JWT.
utilities.tool.jwt.errorSegments: A JWT must include at least header and payload segments.
utilities.tool.jwt.errorObject: JWT header and payload must decode into JSON objects.
utilities.tool.regex.label: Open Regex Tester
utilities.tool.regex.description: Test regular expressions with live matches and capture groups.
utilities.tool.regex.title: Regular expression tester
utilities.tool.regex.panelDescription: Compose a pattern and flags, then preview matches against any sample text.
utilities.tool.regex.matchesDescription: Matches update live as you edit the pattern, flags, or test string.
utilities.tool.regex.empty: No matches for the current pattern.
utilities.tool.regex.errorPattern: The pattern or flags are not a valid regular expression.
utilities.tool.regex.errorExecution: Matching failed for the current input.
utilities.tool.color.label: Open Color Converter
utilities.tool.color.description: Convert colors between hex, rgb, and hsl with a live swatch preview.
utilities.tool.color.title: Color converter
utilities.tool.color.panelDescription: Paste any supported color string or use the swatch picker to explore conversions.
utilities.tool.color.outputsDescription: Converted representations update instantly alongside the color preview.
utilities.tool.color.error: The current value is not a recognised color format.
utilities.tool.diff.label: Open Diff Viewer
utilities.tool.diff.description: Compare two text inputs line by line with additions and removals highlighted.
utilities.tool.diff.leftDescription: Paste the baseline text to compare against.
utilities.tool.diff.rightDescription: Paste the revised text to see what changed.
utilities.tool.diff.resultDescription: Added, removed, and unchanged lines update as you edit either side.
utilities.tool.diff.empty: Enter text on either side to compare it.
utilities.tool.diff.truncated: Input was truncated before comparison for performance.

goToSymbol.empty.unsupported: Symbol navigation is only available for JavaScript and TypeScript files.
goToSymbol.empty.noSymbols: No symbols found in this file.
